purpose: Extend the Ananta worker into a Hermes-like, capable agent executor while preserving Ananta's hub-owned policy, approval, context, routing, audit, and artifact-first architecture.
goal: Add skills, memory, provider flexibility, toolsets, context compression, subworkers, scheduled jobs, external-agent adapters, diagnostics, and OpenAI-compatible surfaces to the Ananta worker without allowing the worker to become the control plane or bypass Hub governance.

hermes_like_capabilities_to_adapt: multi-provider runtime including local and OpenAI-compatible endpoints
ananta_specific_constraints: Hub remains owner of Goal -> Plan -> Task -> Execution -> Verification -> Artifact.
Worker executes only delegated tasks within explicit ExecutionEnvelope constraints.
Worker cannot create new authority for itself, its tools, subworkers, models, memory, or adapters.
Every mutation-capable path remains policy-, approval-, context-, and audit-gated.
Every meaningful result must be represented as an artifact with provenance.

non_goals: Do not turn the worker into an independent autonomous controller.
Do not implement worker-to-worker orchestration that bypasses the Hub.
Do not allow skills to auto-install or auto-patch themselves into active use without approval.
Do not let external agents such as Hermes, OpenCode, Aider, ShellGPT, or Copilot CLI own architecture decisions.
Do not add YOLO-style bypass modes for production or strict governance profiles.
Do not send full repository dumps, secrets, private context, or internal data to cloud providers by default.
Do not treat natural-language prompt instructions as security controls.
architecture_guardrails: The Hub is the control plane; the worker is an execution plane.
Policy is evaluated before execution and defaults to deny when missing, malformed, stale, or ambiguous.
Capabilities are explicit, immutable per execution, and never inferred from user prompt text.
Context is explicit, bounded, provenance-aware, sensitivity-aware, and token-budgeted.
Tool calls, model calls, memory writes, scheduled jobs, adapter invocations, and subworker spawns require typed envelopes.
Every side effect is traceable through TraceBundle and artifacts.
Adapters are untrusted until their output is parsed, validated, sanitized, and converted to Ananta artifacts.
Cloud use is a policy decision, not a worker convenience fallback.
Dangerous or mutation-capable work must fail closed if approval or audit cannot be established.

milestones.title: Governed skill system and safe self-improvement
Scheduled worker jobs under Hub governance

tasks.title: Create ADR for Ananta Worker as governed agent executor
tasks.objective: Define the worker as a Hermes-like execution engine that remains subordinate to the Ananta Hub.
acceptance_criteria: ADR states that Hub owns Goal -> Plan -> Task -> Execution -> Verification -> Artifact.
ADR explicitly rejects worker-owned routing, approval, policy, and unrestricted memory mutation.
ADR maps Hermes reference concepts to Ananta-owned equivalents: skills, memory, toolsets, provider routing, subagents, cron, context compression.
ADR defines stable terms: Hub, Worker, Capability, ContextEnvelope, TraceBundle, Artifact, ToolRouterDecision, ApprovalDecision.
test_requirements: Negative test verifies fail-closed behavior where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not let worker infer missing policy from prompt text.
Do not encode capability checks only in natural-language system prompts.

tasks.title: Define worker responsibility matrix
tasks.objective: Document what the worker may decide locally and what must always come from Hub.
acceptance_criteria: Matrix separates local execution decisions from hub-owned policy decisions.
Worker may choose local implementation mechanics only inside an already approved ExecutionEnvelope.
Worker must not expand allowed tools, context, model provider, filesystem scope, network scope, or cloud access.
Matrix is referenced by docs and tests to prevent architecture drift.
test_requirements: Negative test verifies fail-closed behavior where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not let worker infer missing policy from prompt text.
Do not encode capability checks only in natural-language system prompts.

tasks.title: Define canonical ExecutionEnvelope contract
tasks.objective: Create the single request object that every enhanced worker execution must receive.
acceptance_criteria: ExecutionEnvelope includes task_id, goal_id, actor_ref, capability_grant, context_envelope_ref, allowed_operations, denied_operations, model_policy, tool_policy, approval_refs, audit_correlation_id, trace_parent_id.
Envelope is validated before any model call, tool call, shell call, patch apply, file write, network access, MCP access, memory write, or subworker spawn.
Missing capability_grant or context_envelope_ref fails closed.
Envelope schema has positive and negative examples.
test_requirements: Negative test verifies fail-closed behavior where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not let worker infer missing policy from prompt text.
Do not encode capability checks only in natural-language system prompts.

tasks.title: Define canonical WorkerResult contract
tasks.objective: Create the single result object returned by enhanced workers.
acceptance_criteria: WorkerResult includes status, summary, artifacts, trace_bundle, policy_observations, follow_up_tasks, warnings, degraded_state, and explicit no_side_effects_confirmed flag where applicable.
All side effects are represented as artifacts or trace entries.
WorkerResult distinguishes success, partial_success, denied, needs_approval, failed, degraded, and invalid_request.
Plain text-only responses are not accepted for task execution endpoints.
test_requirements: Negative test verifies fail-closed behavior where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not let worker infer missing policy from prompt text.
Do not encode capability checks only in natural-language system prompts.

tasks.title: Define capability vocabulary and risk classes
tasks.objective: Normalize capability classes used by Hub, ToolRouter, Worker and Approval.
acceptance_criteria: Capability vocabulary covers planning, research, code_read, patch_propose, patch_apply, shell_plan, shell_execute, test_run, verify, memory_read, memory_write, mcp_call, provider_call, subworker_spawn, cron_schedule, artifact_publish.
Every capability has risk_class, approval_default, context_requirements, side_effect_type, and audit_requirements.
Vocabulary is consumable by Hub policy and Worker validation code.
Unknown capability class is rejected, not treated as low risk.
test_requirements: Negative test verifies fail-closed behavior where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not let worker infer missing policy from prompt text.
Do not encode capability checks only in natural-language system prompts.

tasks.title: Define migration boundary for existing worker modes
tasks.objective: Map existing plan_only/patch_propose/patch_apply/command_plan/command_execute/test_run/verify modes onto the new envelope model.
acceptance_criteria: Each existing worker mode maps to one or more capability classes.
Legacy calls are wrapped into ExecutionEnvelope during migration instead of bypassing validation.
Backward compatibility plan names deprecation stages and logging warnings.
Existing tests continue to pass through compatibility adapter.
test_requirements: Negative test verifies fail-closed behavior where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not let worker infer missing policy from prompt text.
Do not encode capability checks only in natural-language system prompts.

tasks.title: Implement ExecutionEnvelope Pydantic models
tasks.objective: Turn the envelope contract into typed runtime validation.
acceptance_criteria: Typed models exist for ExecutionEnvelope, CapabilityGrant, ModelPolicy, ToolPolicy, FilesystemScope, NetworkScope, ApprovalRef, AuditRef.
Validation rejects empty task_id, missing capability_grant, unknown capability classes, and malformed scope rules.
Validation errors are structured and safe to return to Hub.
Model serialization is stable for audit logs and API use.
test_requirements: pytest unit coverage for positive and negative path.
At least one integration-style test through worker route.
anti_patterns: Do not duplicate policy logic separately in each adapter.
Do not rely on prompt instructions as enforcement.

tasks.title: Implement worker preflight gate
tasks.objective: Add a mandatory gate before every execution path.
acceptance_criteria: All public worker execution entrypoints call the same preflight gate.
Preflight verifies envelope validity, capability presence, context availability, model policy, tool policy, approval state, and degraded runtime state.
Preflight returns allow, confirm_required, blocked, or invalid_request with reason_code.
No model/tool/shell/file/network action occurs before preflight success.
test_requirements: pytest unit coverage for positive and negative path.
At least one integration-style test through worker route.
anti_patterns: Do not duplicate policy logic separately in each adapter.
Do not rely on prompt instructions as enforcement.

tasks.title: Add fail-closed default-deny behavior
tasks.objective: Ensure missing or ambiguous policy denies execution.
acceptance_criteria: Unknown task_kind is denied or downgraded to plan_only without mutation.
Unknown tool id is denied.
Unknown provider id is denied.
Missing approval for approval-required operation returns needs_approval and does not execute.
Unit tests prove deny-by-default for missing policy, malformed policy, and stale approval ref.
test_requirements: pytest unit coverage for positive and negative path.
At least one integration-style test through worker route.
anti_patterns: Do not duplicate policy logic separately in each adapter.
Do not rely on prompt instructions as enforcement.

tasks.title: Implement immutable capability snapshot per task
tasks.objective: Prevent mid-run capability escalation.
acceptance_criteria: Worker stores a per-execution immutable capability snapshot from the envelope.
Runtime code reads capabilities only from the snapshot, not from mutable config or prompt output.
Substeps cannot add capabilities; they can only consume existing ones.
TraceBundle includes the capability snapshot hash.
test_requirements: pytest unit coverage for positive and negative path.
At least one integration-style test through worker route.
anti_patterns: Do not duplicate policy logic separately in each adapter.
Do not rely on prompt instructions as enforcement.

tasks.title: Implement policy decision reason codes
tasks.objective: Make denial and approval outcomes machine-readable.
acceptance_criteria: Reason codes include missing_capability, denied_operation, approval_required, approval_missing, scope_violation, provider_blocked, cloud_blocked, network_blocked, context_missing, tool_unavailable, degraded_runtime.
Reason codes appear in WorkerResult, TraceBundle, and logs.
Human-readable messages are generated from codes without hiding the code.
Tests assert stable reason codes.
test_requirements: pytest unit coverage for positive and negative path.
At least one integration-style test through worker route.
anti_patterns: Do not duplicate policy logic separately in each adapter.
Do not rely on prompt instructions as enforcement.

tasks.title: Add policy bypass regression tests
tasks.objective: Lock down known bypass routes.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover direct command_execute without envelope.
Tests cover prompt asking worker to ignore policy.
Tests cover tool adapter trying to call shell directly.
Tests cover patch_apply without approval.
Tests cover cloud provider selection when cloudAllowed=false.
Tests cover MCP tool access without mcp_call capability.
test_requirements: pytest unit coverage for positive and negative path.
At least one integration-style test through worker route.
anti_patterns: Do not duplicate policy logic separately in each adapter.
Do not rely on prompt instructions as enforcement.

tasks.title: Create WorkerToolRegistry
tasks.objective: Introduce a controlled registry for all worker tools.
acceptance_criteria: Registry entries declare id, kind, capability_classes, risk_class, input_schema, output_schema, side_effect_type, supports_stateful_session, requires_approval.
Tools cannot be invoked unless present in registry and allowed by ToolPolicy.
Registry can produce a normalized capability catalog for Hub ToolRouter.
Unknown tool ids fail with tool_unknown.
test_requirements: Security regression tests for denied side effects.
anti_patterns: Do not expose arbitrary Python functions as tools automatically.
Do not pass full tool stdout into LLM context without sanitizer and budget checks.

tasks.title: Create ToolInvocationEnvelope
tasks.objective: Wrap every tool call with policy and trace metadata.
acceptance_criteria: ToolInvocationEnvelope includes execution_id, tool_id, arguments, capability_ref, context_refs, approval_ref, timeout, resource_limits, audit_correlation_id.
Envelope validates argument schema before invocation.
Tool calls return ToolResult with stdout/stderr/files/patches/artifacts separated.
ToolResult never hides side effects in free text.
test_requirements: Security regression tests for denied side effects.
anti_patterns: Do not expose arbitrary Python functions as tools automatically.
Do not pass full tool stdout into LLM context without sanitizer and budget checks.

tasks.title: Harden shell command planning vs execution split
tasks.objective: Keep shell planning safe and execution approval-gated.
acceptance_criteria: command_plan produces CommandPlanArtifact only.
command_execute requires shell_execute capability and approval when policy says confirm_required.
Command execution refuses commands outside workspace or environment constraints.
Commands are classified before execution and classification is included in trace.
test_requirements: Security regression tests for denied side effects.
anti_patterns: Do not expose arbitrary Python functions as tools automatically.
Do not pass full tool stdout into LLM context without sanitizer and budget checks.

tasks.title: Harden file read/write and patch paths
tasks.objective: Prevent direct uncontrolled file mutation.
acceptance_criteria: read_file requires code_read or file_read capability and workspace scope match.
patch_propose produces PatchArtifact without modifying main tree.
patch_apply requires patch_apply capability and approval when required.
Direct write_file-like mutation outside patch_apply path is blocked unless explicit capability exists.
Path traversal and symlink escape tests are added.
test_requirements: Security regression tests for denied side effects.
anti_patterns: Do not expose arbitrary Python functions as tools automatically.
Do not pass full tool stdout into LLM context without sanitizer and budget checks.

tasks.title: Add tool output sanitizer and secret redaction
tasks.objective: Prevent leaking secrets from tool output into model context, logs, artifacts, or cloud calls.
acceptance_criteria: Sanitizer masks API keys, tokens, Authorization headers, private keys, .env values, DB passwords, SSH material, and long opaque secrets.
Sanitizer runs before model-visible output, audit log output, UI output, and artifact publication.
Redaction preserves enough shape for debugging without exposing secret value.
Tests include representative secret formats and false-positive sanity cases.
test_requirements: Security regression tests for denied side effects.
anti_patterns: Do not expose arbitrary Python functions as tools automatically.
Do not pass full tool stdout into LLM context without sanitizer and budget checks.

tasks.title: Add resource limits per tool invocation
tasks.objective: Bound CPU/time/output/size to keep workers stable.
acceptance_criteria: ToolInvocationEnvelope supports timeout_seconds, max_output_chars, max_artifact_bytes, max_files_touched, max_processes where applicable.
Timeouts return degraded or failed with partial output and reason_code.
Large output is summarized or stored as bounded artifact, not injected raw into model context.
Defaults are safe when no limit is specified by caller.
test_requirements: Security regression tests for denied side effects.
anti_patterns: Do not expose arbitrary Python functions as tools automatically.
Do not pass full tool stdout into LLM context without sanitizer and budget checks.

tasks.title: Implement tool adapter trust boundary
tasks.objective: Treat external CLIs as untrusted adapters.
acceptance_criteria: Aider/OpenCode/ShellGPT/Copilot/Hermes adapter output is parsed into Ananta artifacts before use.
Adapter cannot directly report success without structured artifact validation.
Adapter cannot bypass policy by doing its own file writes unless the wrapper uses an approved apply path.
Adapter failure states are explicit: parse_error, denied, degraded, tool_unavailable, unsafe_output.
test_requirements: Security regression tests for denied side effects.
anti_patterns: Do not expose arbitrary Python functions as tools automatically.
Do not pass full tool stdout into LLM context without sanitizer and budget checks.

tasks.title: Create WorkerProviderRegistry
tasks.objective: Add Hermes-like provider flexibility behind Ananta policy.
acceptance_criteria: Registry supports ollama, lmstudio, openai_compatible, openai, openrouter, anthropic, local_mock, and custom endpoints as entries.
Each provider entry declares local_or_cloud, credential_source, supports_tools, supports_streaming, context_limit, cost_class, risk_class.
Provider list can be returned to Hub without exposing secrets.
Unknown providers are denied.
test_requirements: Mock endpoint tests for credential scoping and cloud blocking.
anti_patterns: Do not let environment variables silently override saved policy.
Do not implement fallback by trying every configured cloud key.

tasks.title: Implement provider selection as Hub decision input
tasks.objective: Prevent worker-owned provider escalation.
acceptance_criteria: Worker accepts selected provider/model only through ExecutionEnvelope or ToolRouterDecision.
Worker may report provider availability but does not select cloud provider on its own.
Fallback providers are allowed only if envelope permits fallback and target is policy-compatible.
Cloud provider use is blocked when model_policy.cloud_allowed=false.
test_requirements: Mock endpoint tests for credential scoping and cloud blocking.
anti_patterns: Do not let environment variables silently override saved policy.
Do not implement fallback by trying every configured cloud key.

tasks.title: Add local-first model routing support
tasks.objective: Make local Ollama/LM Studio practical for worker tasks.
acceptance_criteria: Local provider adapters support health check, model list where available, generate/chat call, timeout, and clear error mapping.
Adapters do not require API keys for local endpoints unless configured.
Connection failure returns provider_unavailable not generic crash.
Smoke test works against mock OpenAI-compatible local server.
test_requirements: Mock endpoint tests for credential scoping and cloud blocking.
anti_patterns: Do not let environment variables silently override saved policy.
Do not implement fallback by trying every configured cloud key.

tasks.title: Add provider credential isolation
tasks.objective: Ensure provider secrets do not leak to tools or wrong endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: Provider API keys are scoped to provider id and base_url.
OpenAI key is not sent to OpenRouter, LM Studio, Ollama, or arbitrary custom endpoint unless explicitly configured.
Worker tool subprocesses do not inherit provider secrets by default.
Trace logs contain credential_source but never key material.
test_requirements: Mock endpoint tests for credential scoping and cloud blocking.
anti_patterns: Do not let environment variables silently override saved policy.
Do not implement fallback by trying every configured cloud key.

tasks.title: Implement auxiliary model policy
tasks.objective: Allow optional helper model calls without bypassing policy.
acceptance_criteria: Auxiliary tasks are classified: compression, summarization, validation, search_summary, skill_review.
Each auxiliary call uses a model allowed by model_policy.auxiliary_allowed_providers.
If auxiliary call would send sensitive context to cloud while cloud disallowed, it is blocked or forced local.
Auxiliary model output is marked untrusted until validated.
test_requirements: Mock endpoint tests for credential scoping and cloud blocking.
anti_patterns: Do not let environment variables silently override saved policy.
Do not implement fallback by trying every configured cloud key.

tasks.title: Add provider runtime diagnostics endpoint
tasks.objective: Expose safe provider readiness for operators.
acceptance_criteria: Endpoint returns provider id, availability, model count or status, local/cloud classification, and error_code.
Endpoint never returns secrets, raw headers, full env vars, or private tokens.
Diagnostics distinguish unavailable, unauthorized, misconfigured, timeout, and blocked_by_policy.
Dashboard/TUI can consume the structure.
test_requirements: Mock endpoint tests for credential scoping and cloud blocking.
anti_patterns: Do not let environment variables silently override saved policy.
Do not implement fallback by trying every configured cloud key.

tasks.title: Implement ContextEnvelope resolver in worker
tasks.objective: Make context explicit, bounded, and provenance-aware.
acceptance_criteria: Worker receives context references from Hub and resolves only allowed references.
Context blocks include source_type, origin_id, provenance, sensitivity, token_estimate, hash.
Worker refuses unbounded repo dump or raw all-files context.
Final context bundle records included, compacted, and dropped blocks with reason codes.
test_requirements: Injection scanner regression tests where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not inject entire repository into prompt.
Do not let the LLM decide which sensitive files are safe.

tasks.title: Implement token budget enforcement
tasks.objective: Avoid uncontrolled context growth.
acceptance_criteria: Budget is enforced globally and per source class.
P0 control signals are never silently dropped.
Low-priority overflow is compacted first and truncated last.
WorkerResult includes context budget requested/used and compaction actions.
test_requirements: Injection scanner regression tests where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not inject entire repository into prompt.
Do not let the LLM decide which sensitive files are safe.

tasks.title: Implement context compression service
tasks.objective: Add Hermes-like compression but with provenance and policy.
acceptance_criteria: Compression preserves task objective, acceptance criteria, policy constraints, latest failure reasons, and artifact refs.
Compressed blocks include source hashes and do not pretend to be raw source.
Cloud compression is blocked unless cloud_allowed and sensitivity allow it.
Compression output has validation tests for preserving control state.
test_requirements: Injection scanner regression tests where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not inject entire repository into prompt.
Do not let the LLM decide which sensitive files are safe.

tasks.title: Create worker memory stores
tasks.objective: Add bounded memory without letting worker mutate global truth.
acceptance_criteria: Separate stores exist for worker_session_memory, project_execution_memory, and proposed_long_term_memory.
Worker can write session/project memory only when memory_write capability allows it.
Long-term memory updates are proposals unless Hub approval enables direct write.
Memory entries include provenance, source task, sensitivity, created_at, and confidence.
test_requirements: Injection scanner regression tests where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not inject entire repository into prompt.
Do not let the LLM decide which sensitive files are safe.

tasks.title: Add session search over worker traces and artifacts
tasks.objective: Allow recall of previous executions without prompt dumping.
acceptance_criteria: Search can find prior tasks, failures, patch artifacts, verification artifacts, and decisions.
Search result snippets are bounded and cite artifact/trace ids.
Search respects project/tenant/scope filters.
Search cannot return data from unrelated project scope.
test_requirements: Injection scanner regression tests where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not inject entire repository into prompt.
Do not let the LLM decide which sensitive files are safe.

tasks.title: Add prompt injection scanning for context blocks
tasks.objective: Protect model calls from malicious repo/docs content.
acceptance_criteria: Context blocks are scanned for prompt injection, secret exfiltration instructions, hidden unicode, suspicious HTML comments, and tool override attempts.
Blocked blocks are replaced by safe warning stubs with provenance.
Findings appear in TraceBundle and WorkerResult warnings.
Policy can choose block, warn, or require approval depending on source and governance mode.
test_requirements: Injection scanner regression tests where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not inject entire repository into prompt.
Do not let the LLM decide which sensitive files are safe.

tasks.title: Define Ananta SkillManifest schema
tasks.objective: Introduce Hermes-like skills as governed, versioned worker modules.
acceptance_criteria: SkillManifest includes id, name, version, description, capability_requirements, allowed_tools, denied_tools, context_requirements, inputs_schema, outputs_schema, risk_class, required_approvals, test_fixtures.
Skill cannot declare capabilities not granted by Hub at runtime.
Skill manifests are machine-validated before load.
Invalid skills fail closed with reason_code skill_invalid.
test_requirements: Fixture tests for baseline skills where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not allow autonomous skill auto-install by default.
Do not store skill instructions only as prompt snippets without manifest metadata.

tasks.title: Implement SkillRegistry with signed or hashed skills
tasks.objective: Make skill loading deterministic and auditable.
acceptance_criteria: Registry records skill id, version, source path/package, content_hash, enabled state, compatibility range.
Skill updates change content_hash and require reload.
TraceBundle records skill ids and hashes used in execution.
Optional signature field is supported or explicitly deferred with documented hash baseline.
test_requirements: Fixture tests for baseline skills where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not allow autonomous skill auto-install by default.
Do not store skill instructions only as prompt snippets without manifest metadata.

tasks.title: Implement SkillRunner
tasks.objective: Execute skills through the same policy/tool/context gates.
acceptance_criteria: SkillRunner receives ExecutionEnvelope and never creates its own authority.
Skill tool calls go through WorkerToolRegistry and ToolInvocationEnvelope.
Skill output is converted into WorkerResult artifacts.
Skill cannot mutate memory, files, tools, or provider settings unless the envelope grants it.
test_requirements: Fixture tests for baseline skills where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not allow autonomous skill auto-install by default.
Do not store skill instructions only as prompt snippets without manifest metadata.

tasks.title: Add skill proposal workflow
tasks.objective: Allow self-improvement only as reviewable proposals.
acceptance_criteria: After complex tasks, worker may produce SkillProposalArtifact if skill_propose capability is granted.
Skill proposals include rationale, trigger examples, manifest, implementation sketch, tests, risk analysis, and required capabilities.
Skill proposals are not auto-enabled.
Hub or operator approval is required before skill install/enable.
test_requirements: Fixture tests for baseline skills where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not allow autonomous skill auto-install by default.
Do not store skill instructions only as prompt snippets without manifest metadata.

tasks.title: Add skill self-review and stale-skill detection
tasks.objective: Let worker critique skills without silently patching them.
acceptance_criteria: Worker can produce SkillReviewArtifact with outdated, overlapping, unsafe, or failing-skill findings.
Skill patch suggestions are PatchArtifact or SkillProposalArtifact, not direct writes.
Pinned/core skills cannot be modified by worker without explicit admin approval.
Reviews cite executions or tests that justify the recommendation.
test_requirements: Fixture tests for baseline skills where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not allow autonomous skill auto-install by default.
Do not store skill instructions only as prompt snippets without manifest metadata.

tasks.title: Add baseline coding/review/diagnose skills
tasks.objective: Seed useful worker skills matching Ananta use cases.
acceptance_criteria: Baseline skills cover repository_understand, bugfix_plan, patch_propose, change_review, compose_diagnose, test_failure_triage, release_check.
Each baseline skill has fixtures and expected artifacts.
Each skill declares minimal capabilities.
No baseline skill requires unrestricted shell or network access.
test_requirements: Fixture tests for baseline skills where applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not allow autonomous skill auto-install by default.
Do not store skill instructions only as prompt snippets without manifest metadata.

tasks.title: Define SubworkerEnvelope contract
tasks.objective: Allow Hermes-like delegation without worker-to-worker power escalation.
acceptance_criteria: SubworkerEnvelope includes parent_execution_id, delegated_task, reduced_capability_grant, context_subset, timeout, max_depth, audit_correlation_id.
Subworker capability set must be subset of parent capabilities.
Subworker context set must be subset or policy-approved derivative of parent context.
Depth limit and concurrency limit are enforced.
anti_patterns: Do not let subworkers call Hub as independent actors unless explicitly registered.
Do not delegate mutation tasks concurrently by default.

tasks.title: Implement subworker spawn gate
tasks.objective: Route all child work through Hub-compatible policy checks.
acceptance_criteria: Subworker spawn requires subworker_spawn capability.
Spawn gate rejects missing reduced capability grant.
Spawn gate blocks cycles, unbounded fan-out, and recursive self-delegation beyond limit.
Spawn result is represented as artifacts and trace children.
anti_patterns: Do not let subworkers call Hub as independent actors unless explicitly registered.
Do not delegate mutation tasks concurrently by default.

tasks.title: Implement parallel safe execution coordinator
tasks.objective: Support limited parallelism without nondeterministic side effects.
acceptance_criteria: Parallel tasks are allowed only for read-only, independent, or explicitly isolated operations.
Patch/apply/write/shell mutation tasks are serialized unless policy marks them independent.
Coordinator preserves deterministic result ordering.
Failures in one child do not hide failures or side effects in others.
anti_patterns: Do not let subworkers call Hub as independent actors unless explicitly registered.
Do not delegate mutation tasks concurrently by default.

tasks.title: Add delegation artifact model
tasks.objective: Make delegated work reviewable.
acceptance_criteria: DelegationArtifact records child task objective, capabilities, context refs, result status, artifacts, and trace ids.
Parent WorkerResult summarizes child outcomes without flattening away details.
Audit can reconstruct delegation tree.
UI/TUI can display delegation tree from artifact data.
anti_patterns: Do not let subworkers call Hub as independent actors unless explicitly registered.
Do not delegate mutation tasks concurrently by default.

tasks.title: Add cancellation and timeout propagation
tasks.objective: Make long-running worker/subworker runs controllable.
acceptance_criteria: Cancellation request stops future tool calls and attempts safe interrupt for current process.
Timeout propagates to children.
WorkerResult marks cancellation vs timeout vs failure distinctly.
TraceBundle records cancellation source and completed side effects before stop.
anti_patterns: Do not let subworkers call Hub as independent actors unless explicitly registered.
Do not delegate mutation tasks concurrently by default.

tasks.title: Add OpenAI-compatible worker facade
tasks.objective: Expose worker capabilities to compatible clients without bypassing Ananta controls.
acceptance_criteria: Facade supports /v1/chat/completions for allowed interactive worker use cases.
Facade requires an Ananta capability token or Hub-issued session context.
Facade maps chat requests to ExecutionEnvelope or rejects them.
Facade exposes clear unsupported errors for unrestricted tool calls.
test_requirements: Policy bypass tests for external adapters.
anti_patterns: Do not expose raw worker tool API publicly.
Do not translate arbitrary chat prompt into unrestricted shell permissions.

tasks.title: Add Ananta-native worker RPC endpoint
tasks.objective: Provide first-class structured execution API.
acceptance_criteria: Endpoint accepts ExecutionEnvelope and returns WorkerResult.
Endpoint validates schema before execution.
Endpoint supports sync and async/job mode if existing architecture supports it.
Endpoint returns stable error bodies for invalid_request, denied, needs_approval, failed, degraded.
test_requirements: Policy bypass tests for external adapters.
anti_patterns: Do not expose raw worker tool API publicly.
Do not translate arbitrary chat prompt into unrestricted shell permissions.

tasks.title: Build Hermes adapter as external worker target
tasks.objective: Allow Hermes to be plugged in as a worker/backend without becoming owner.
acceptance_criteria: Adapter sends bounded task/context to Hermes only when policy allows.
Adapter strips or redacts context according to ContextEnvelope sensitivity.
Adapter parses Hermes response into Ananta artifacts and trace.
Adapter blocks Hermes tool autonomy unless sandboxed and explicitly allowed by capability.
test_requirements: Policy bypass tests for external adapters.
anti_patterns: Do not expose raw worker tool API publicly.
Do not translate arbitrary chat prompt into unrestricted shell permissions.

tasks.title: Build OpenCode adapter boundary hardening
tasks.objective: Treat OpenCode as an execution adapter, not controller.
acceptance_criteria: OpenCode invocation receives only allowed files/context.
OpenCode patch output is parsed as PatchArtifact.
OpenCode cannot directly write into main tree unless patch_apply path is approved.
Adapter records command line, version, cwd, input hash, and output hash.
test_requirements: Policy bypass tests for external adapters.
anti_patterns: Do not expose raw worker tool API publicly.
Do not translate arbitrary chat prompt into unrestricted shell permissions.

tasks.title: Add MCP adapter with capability filtering
tasks.objective: Allow MCP tools only through Ananta policy.
acceptance_criteria: MCP server/tool list is filtered by ToolPolicy.
MCP environment variables are explicit allowlist only.
MCP tool results are sanitized before model-visible use.
Private/internal URL and credential-bearing MCP calls require explicit capability.
test_requirements: Policy bypass tests for external adapters.
anti_patterns: Do not expose raw worker tool API publicly.
Do not translate arbitrary chat prompt into unrestricted shell permissions.

tasks.title: Add API exposure policy and self-loop guard
tasks.objective: Prevent Ananta worker API from recursive unsafe calls.
acceptance_criteria: API exposure is disabled or minimal by default unless config enables it.
Requests include X-Ananta-Instance-ID and hop count or equivalent internal loop guard.
Self-calls beyond configured hop limit are blocked.
Exposure policy appears in diagnostics and config read-model.
test_requirements: Policy bypass tests for external adapters.
anti_patterns: Do not expose raw worker tool API publicly.
Do not translate arbitrary chat prompt into unrestricted shell permissions.

tasks.title: Implement TraceBundle v2
tasks.objective: Make all worker actions reconstructable.
acceptance_criteria: TraceBundle includes execution_id, task_id, goal_id, actor_ref, capability_hash, context_hash, model_calls, tool_calls, approvals, artifacts, timings, warnings, degraded states.
TraceBundle is produced for success, denial, failure, timeout, and cancellation.
TraceBundle avoids secret values and raw sensitive payloads.
TraceBundle can be correlated with Hub audit records.
anti_patterns: Do not log secrets for observability.
Do not make audit optional for mutation paths.

tasks.title: Add model call trace with privacy controls
tasks.objective: Record enough model metadata for debugging without leaking prompts.
acceptance_criteria: Trace records provider_id, model, local_or_cloud, token estimates, latency, retry count, fallback use, and context hashes.
Raw prompt/response logging is disabled by default.
Optional debug logging is scope-limited and redacted.
Cloud calls record sensitivity classification and policy reason.
anti_patterns: Do not log secrets for observability.
Do not make audit optional for mutation paths.

tasks.title: Add artifact-first execution enforcement
tasks.objective: Ensure all meaningful output is an artifact.
acceptance_criteria: Plans are PlanArtifact, patches are PatchArtifact, commands are CommandPlanArtifact/CommandResultArtifact, tests are TestResultArtifact, reviews are VerificationArtifact/ReviewArtifact.
WorkerResult free-text summary references artifact ids.
Execution path fails tests if it returns only free text for non-trivial tasks.
Artifacts include provenance and content hash.
anti_patterns: Do not log secrets for observability.
Do not make audit optional for mutation paths.

tasks.title: Add operator diagnostics for worker state
tasks.objective: Expose health, capabilities, and degradation safely.
acceptance_criteria: Diagnostics include worker_id, version, runtime mode, registered tools, registered providers, enabled skill count, queue state if applicable, and last health errors.
Diagnostics include policy summary but not secrets.
Diagnostics include degraded subsystems with reason codes.
Output is consumable by dashboard/TUI.
anti_patterns: Do not log secrets for observability.
Do not make audit optional for mutation paths.

tasks.title: Add audit event emission for every sensitive step
tasks.objective: Keep security-relevant actions visible.
acceptance_criteria: Audit events emitted for preflight decision, approval_required, approval_consumed, policy_denied, shell_execute, patch_apply, file_write, provider_cloud_call, memory_write, mcp_call, subworker_spawn.
Audit events include correlation id and reason code.
Audit event emission failure fails closed for mutation-heavy operations or records degraded_state according to policy.
Tests verify events for allowed and denied paths.
anti_patterns: Do not log secrets for observability.
Do not make audit optional for mutation paths.

tasks.title: Define scheduled worker job contract
tasks.objective: Add Hermes-like cron safely under Hub control.
acceptance_criteria: Scheduled jobs are owned by Hub, not worker local memory.
Job contract includes schedule, capability_grant, context policy, max_runtime, approval mode, delivery target, and audit correlation.
Worker cannot create persistent scheduled jobs unless schedule_create capability is granted.
Jobs are visible and revocable from Hub/operator surface.
anti_patterns: Do not let worker create hidden cron files.
Do not run dangerous commands unattended because user is absent.

tasks.title: Implement headless approval behavior
tasks.objective: Prevent unattended dangerous execution.
acceptance_criteria: Headless jobs cannot execute confirm_required actions without pre-approved approval_ref.
Blocked actions remain blocked in headless mode.
Approval expiry is enforced.
WorkerResult marks skipped steps due to missing approval instead of pretending success.
anti_patterns: Do not let worker create hidden cron files.
Do not run dangerous commands unattended because user is absent.

tasks.title: Add scheduled job result artifacts
tasks.objective: Make recurring work reviewable.
acceptance_criteria: Each run produces JobRunArtifact with status, started_at, ended_at, artifacts, trace_bundle_ref, warnings, and next_run if known.
Failed jobs include reason_code and retry recommendation.
Output delivery never becomes the only storage of results.
Job history is queryable by job_id and task/goal context.
anti_patterns: Do not let worker create hidden cron files.
Do not run dangerous commands unattended because user is absent.

tasks.title: Create worker security regression suite
tasks.objective: Collect all bypass tests into a maintained suite.
acceptance_criteria: Suite covers policy bypass, context bypass, cloud bypass, tool bypass, shell bypass, patch bypass, memory bypass, adapter bypass, MCP bypass, and subworker escalation.
Suite runs in CI standard check or named security job.
Each test has a short comment naming the threat it prevents.
New worker capabilities require adding at least one positive and one negative test.
test_requirements: CI or smoke test listed in guide.
anti_patterns: Do not mark feature complete without negative tests.
Do not hide breaking changes behind silent compatibility behavior.

tasks.title: Create live local worker smoke test
tasks.objective: Prove local-first path works end-to-end.
acceptance_criteria: Smoke starts Hub and Worker or uses existing local-dev mode.
Smoke submits plan_only and patch_propose tasks using local/mock provider.
Smoke verifies WorkerResult, artifacts, TraceBundle, and no unauthorized side effects.
Smoke can run without paid cloud APIs.
test_requirements: CI or smoke test listed in guide.
anti_patterns: Do not mark feature complete without negative tests.
Do not hide breaking changes behind silent compatibility behavior.

tasks.title: Create adapter conformance test harness
tasks.objective: Ensure external adapters behave like Ananta workers.
acceptance_criteria: Harness feeds sample tasks to native, OpenCode, Hermes, ShellGPT adapters when available.
Harness verifies output artifacts, denied states, redaction, and no direct mutation.
Unavailable adapters are skipped with explicit reason, not failed obscurely.
Conformance checklist is documented for future adapters.
test_requirements: CI or smoke test listed in guide.
anti_patterns: Do not mark feature complete without negative tests.
Do not hide breaking changes behind silent compatibility behavior.

tasks.title: Write worker extension implementation guide
tasks.objective: Give weaker implementers exact steps and boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Guide explains architecture, file locations, contracts, allowed responsibilities, forbidden shortcuts, and test commands.
Guide includes minimal skeleton code examples for envelope validation, tool invocation, provider adapter, skill runner, and artifact creation.
Guide includes a 'wrong implementation examples' section.
Guide links this todo file and current architecture docs.
test_requirements: CI or smoke test listed in guide.
anti_patterns: Do not mark feature complete without negative tests.
Do not hide breaking changes behind silent compatibility behavior.

tasks.title: Update README and operator docs
tasks.objective: Expose the new worker model to users/operators.
acceptance_criteria: README mentions enhanced worker as governed executor, not autonomous owner.
Docs show how to enable local provider, inspect capabilities, and read artifacts/traces.
Docs state cloud/context safety defaults clearly.
Docs include troubleshooting for denied/degraded worker states.
test_requirements: CI or smoke test listed in guide.
anti_patterns: Do not mark feature complete without negative tests.
Do not hide breaking changes behind silent compatibility behavior.

tasks.title: Add rollout feature flags and migration plan
tasks.objective: Introduce changes without breaking current users.
acceptance_criteria: Feature flags allow enabling envelope_v2, skill_registry, provider_registry, subworkers, cron, and external adapters separately.
Default mode preserves existing safe behavior.
Migration logs warn on legacy calls.
Rollback instructions exist for each major subsystem.
test_requirements: CI or smoke test listed in guide.
anti_patterns: Do not mark feature complete without negative tests.
Do not hide breaking changes behind silent compatibility behavior.

definition_of_done_global: All new worker entrypoints require ExecutionEnvelope or an approved compatibility wrapper.
All mutation-capable paths have positive and negative tests.
All model calls record provider/model/local-or-cloud metadata and obey model_policy.
All tool calls go through WorkerToolRegistry and ToolInvocationEnvelope.
All external adapter results are parsed into Ananta artifacts before being trusted.
All context bundles include provenance, budget accounting, and sensitivity handling.
All denial states are explicit and machine-readable.
CI contains a security regression suite for known bypass classes.
Operator documentation explains how to use, diagnose, and safely extend the worker.
summary_notes: This track intentionally borrows Hermes-like productivity concepts but keeps the Ananta worker subordinate to Hub policy and artifacts.

A weaker implementation should start with ExecutionEnvelope, WorkerResult, preflight gate, fail-closed policy, ToolRegistry, ProviderRegistry, ContextEnvelope, TraceBundle, and regression tests before adding skills or subworkers.
The worker may become smarter and more capable, but never more authoritative than the Hub-issued capability grant.
